title: 第 36 節
en: 3 And as its ether, i.e. as the ether in the heart, the Brahman, with which prawa is connected. Comm.
4 The household altar.
[3] F 66 ATtfANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.
forms of Brahman). The person that is seen in the sun, I am he, I am he indeed 1.

2. ' He who knowing this meditates on him, destroys sin, obtains the world (of Agni Garhapatya), reaches his full age, and lives long ; his descendants do not perish. We guard him in this world and in the other, whosoever knowing this meditates on him.'
Twelfth Khaatz>a.
i. Then the Anvaharya fire2 taught him: ' Water, the quarters, the stars, the moon (these are my forms). The person that is seen in the moon, I am he, I am he indeed.

2. ' He who knowing this meditates on him, destroys sin, obtains the world (of Agni Anvaharya), reaches his full age, and lives long ; his descendants do not perish. We guard him in this world and in the other, whosoever knowing this meditates on him.'
Thirteenth Khanda.
A i. Then the Ahavaniya3 fire taught him : ' Breath, ether, heaven, and lightning (these are my forms). The person that is seen in the lightning, I am he, I am he indeed.

1 Fanciful similarities and relations between the fires of the three altars and their various forms and manifestations are pointed out by the commentator. Thus earth and food are represented as warmed and boiled by the fire. The sun is said to give warmth and light like the fire of the altar. The chief point, however, is that in all of them Brahman is manifested.
2 The altar on the right. Anvaharya is a sacrificial oblation, chiefly one intended for the manes.
3 The Ahavaniya altar is the altar on the eastern side of the sacrificial ground.

IV PRAPAFffAKA, 1 5 KHAiVDA, I. 67 2. ' He who knowing this meditates on him, destroys sin, obtains the world (of Agni Ahavaniya), reaches his full age, and lives long ; his descendants do not perish. We guard him in this world and in the other, whosoever knowing this meditates on him.'
Fourteenth Khanda.
1. Then they all said: ' Upakosala, this is our knowledge, our friend, and the knowledge of the Self, but the teacher will tell you the way (to another life).'
2. In time his teacher came back, and said to him: ' Upakoi"ala.' He answered: 'Sir.' The teacher said : ' Friend, your face shines like that of one who knows Brahman. Who has taught you ?'
' Who should teach me, Sir ?' he said. He denies, as it were. And he said (pointing) to the fires : 'Are these fires other than fires?'
The teacher said: 'What, my friend, have these fires told you ?'
3. He answered: 'This' (repeating some of what they had told him).

The teacher said : ' My friend, they have taught you about the worlds, but I shall tell you this ; and as water does not cling to a lotus leaf, so no evil deed clings to one who knows it.' He said: 'Sir, tell it me.'
Fifteenth Kkanda.

1. He said : ' The person that is seen in the eye, that is the Self. This is the immortal, the fearless, this is Brahman1. Even though they drop melted 1 This is also the teaching of Pra^apati in VIII, 7, 4.
butter or water on him, it runs away on both sides *.
2. 'They call him Sawyadvama, for all blessings (vama) go towards him (sa/;zyanti). All blessings go towards him who knows this.
3. 'He is also Vamani, for he leads (nayati) all blessings (vama). He leads all blessings who knows this.
4. ' He is also Bhamani, for he shines (bhati) in all worlds. He who knows this, shines in all worlds.

5. ' Now (if one who knows this, dies), whether people perform obsequies for him or no, he goes to light (ar/£is) 2, from light to day, from day to the light half of the moon, from the light half of the moon to the six months during which the sun goes to the north, from the months to the year, from the year to the sun, from the sun to the moon, from the moon to the lightning. There is a person not human, 6. ' He leads them to Brahman. This is the path of the Devas, the path that leads to Brahman. Those who proceed on that path, do not return to the life of man, yea, they do not return.'
Sixteenth Khaa^da3.
1. Verily, he who purifies (Vayu) is the sacrifice, for he (the air) moving along, purifies everything.
zh: 而作為它的以太，即作為心中的以太，梵天（Brahman）與此相連；造句力（prana）與之相連。注釋。
4 家庭祭壇
婆羅門的形式）。那在太陽中被看見的人格，我就是他，我確實就是他¹。
2. 「知此而瞑想於彼者，消滅罪惡，獲得（阿格尼‧迦爾哈帕蒂亞之）世界，達其圓滿之年，長壽而生；其子孫不絕。凡知此而瞑想於彼者，我等於此世及彼世皆護衛之。」
第十二個卡塔（Khaatz>a）。
i. 那時安瓦哈里亞火焰教導他：「水、四方、眾星、月亮（這些是我的形式）。月亮中所見之人，我即是他，我確實即是他。
2. 「誰若知此而於彼冥想者，乃摧毀罪惡，獲得彼世界（阿格尼‧安瓦哈里亞之世界），達其圓滿之壽，而長生不死；其子孫不至滅絕。凡知此而於彼冥想者，我等於此世與彼世皆護衛之。」
第十三章。
阿哈瓦尼亞³火祭火教導他說：「氣息、乙太、天界及閃電（這些乃吾之諸形）。閃電中所見之人格，我即是彼，吾誠然即是彼。
評論家指出了三個祭壇之火及其各種形式和顯現之間異想天開的相似性與關係。例如，大地和食物被表現為由火溫暖和煮沸。太陽據說像祭壇之火一樣賦予溫暖和光芒。然而，最重要的一點是，在這一切之中都顯現了梵天（Brahman）。
右邊的祭壇。Anvaharya 是一種祭祀奠祭，主要是為亡靈（manes）而設。
3 阿哈瓦尼亞祭壇（Ahavaniya altar）是犧牲場地東側的祭壇。
IV 普拉帕塔卡（Prapâthaka），1 5 開達（Khanda），I. 67 2. 「凡知此者而冥想於他，則摧毀罪業，獲得世界（阿格尼‧阿哈瓦尼亞〔Agni Ahavaniya〕之世），臻於壽考，而得長壽；其後嗣不至傾頹。凡知此者而冥想於他，我等於此世與彼世俱護衛之。」
第十四卡難達
那時他們都說：「烏帕克薩拉，這是我們的知識，我們的朋友，以及自我的知識，但老師將告訴你通往（另一生命）的道路。」
2. 過了一段時間，他的老師回來了，對他說：「烏帕克瓦拉（Upakoi"ala）。」他回答：「老師。」老師說：「朋友啊，你的面容閃耀著如同認識梵天者的光輝。是誰教導了你？」
「先生，誰該教導我呢？」他說。他予以否定，彷彿。而後他說（指著火焰）：「這些火焰不是火焰嗎？」
教師說：「朋友啊，這些火焰向你說了什麼呢？」
3. 他回答道：『這個』（重複他們之前告訴他的某些內容）。
教師說：「我的朋友，他們已經教過你關於世界的事，但我會告訴你這個；正如水不黏著於蓮葉，同樣地，邪惡的行為不會黏著於知道它的人。」他說：「先生，請告訴我。」
第十五卡達（Fifteenth Kanda）
他說：「眼睛裡所見的那個人，就是自我。這是不朽者，是無畏者，這就是梵天（Brahman）。即使他們澆下融化的 --- *註：原文在此處似乎有所中斷；上文註1提及此亦為梵天之父（Pra^apati）在《奧義書》VIII, 7, 4 中的教導。*
黃油或水倒在他身上，會從兩邊流走*。
2. 「他們稱他為薩維亞多瓦瑪（Sawyadvama），因為一切祝福（vama）都流向他（sa/;zyanti）。凡知此者，一切祝福皆流向之。」
3. 「他也是瓦瑪尼（Vamani），因為他引領（nayati）所有的祝福（vama）。誰知道這一點，他就引領所有的祝福。」
4. 「他亦是婆摩尼（Bhamani），因為他在一切世界中閃耀（bhati）。誰若知曉此事，誰便在一切世界中閃耀。」
5. 『現在（如果了知此道者去世），無論人們是否為他舉行喪禮，他進入光（agnis），從光進入晝，從晝進入月亮的光明半月，從月亮的光明半月進入太陽向北行進的六個月，從月份進入年，從年進入太陽，從太陽進入月亮，從月亮進入閃電。有一個非人的存在，6. 他引導他們進入梵。這是提婆的道路，引向梵的道路。沿著此路前進的人，不再回返於人間的生命，是的，他們不再回返。』
第十六篇章（Khaa^da3）
誠然，淨化（縛由）的人就是祭祀，因為他（空氣）在運動中淨化了一切。
